user.title: Connect Guesty
user.description: Enter your Guesty Open API credentials. You can find the Client ID and Client Secret in your Guesty account under Integrations > API & Webhooks.

reauth_confirm.title: Reconnect Guesty
reauth_confirm.description: The Guesty credentials are no longer valid. Enter a new Client ID and Client Secret.

error.invalid_auth: Invalid API credentials (wrong Client ID or Secret)
error.no_permissions: Token received but no listing access – check Open API permissions in Guesty
error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the Guesty API

init.title: Guesty settings
init.description: Adjust synchronization and optionally enable secure, reservation-scoped door access.

data.expose_guest_details: Show guest names and confirmation codes in entities

access.title: Guest door access
access.description: Create a Guesty reservation custom field of type Text first. The integration writes one protected access link per active reservation. Optional branding images must use direct HTTPS URLs.

data.access_custom_field: Guesty reservation field for the door link (name, variable, or ID)

listing.title: Locks for {listing}
listing.description: Select one to six Home Assistant lock entities and configure their guest-facing labels. Locks two through six are optional and only selected locks appear on the guest page. Known generic door names receive local translation suggestions; every language can be customized. These server-side assignments cannot be changed by a guest link.

loxone.title: Loxone reservation PINs
loxone.description: Generate one six-digit code per reservation and store it in a configurable Guesty reservation custom field. The custom field is the source of truth, so valid manual changes are synchronized to Loxone; duplicate codes are replaced automatically. Loxone users are created only shortly before access begins and are automatically deleted after access ends. The same early/late offsets as the door links are used.

data.loxone_provision_lead_minutes: Create Loxone user before access starts (minutes)
data.loxone_custom_field: Guesty reservation field for the door code (name, variable, or ID)

loxone_server.title: Loxone Miniserver {number}
loxone_server.description: Enter an HTTPS Miniserver or reverse-proxy URL. Saving tests the connection and loads the available user groups. Use a dedicated account with User management rights.

data.name: Display name
data.password: Password (leave empty to keep the current password)

loxone_listing.title: Loxone access for {listing}
loxone_listing.description: Select one or more user groups from exactly one Miniserver. Their permissions determine which doors the reservation PIN can open. The optional Guesty suffix is display-only and is never sent to the lock.

data.guesty_code_suffix: Key shown after the Guesty code (optional)

data_description.guesty_code_suffix: For example #, * or ☑️. Up to 8 visible non-numeric characters; leave empty if the PIN alone is sufficient. This setting is shared with TTLock for the same listing; for different keypads you can enter a combined hint such as ☑️ / #.

ttlock.title: TTLock reservation PINs
ttlock.description: First create an application in [TTLock Open Platform]({open_platform_url}). See [TTLock OAuth help]({oauth_doc_url}) if needed. Use the TTLock App account that manages the locks—not the developer-portal login. Only V4 locks with an online gateway are shown.

data_description.loxone_custom_field: Name, variable, or ID of the Guesty reservation custom field, for example door_code.
data_description.loxone_code_prefix: One or two leading digits for automatically generated six-digit codes, for example 7.
data_description.access_early_minutes: How many minutes before Guesty check-in the code becomes valid.
data_description.access_late_minutes: How many minutes after Guesty check-out the code remains valid.
data_description.ttlock_provision_lead_minutes: How early the code is sent to TTLock. 360 minutes is recommended and keeps distant bookings off the locks.
data_description.region: Usually EU / Europe. It must match the region of your Open Platform application.
data_description.client_id: Copy this from your TTLock Open Platform application.
data_description.client_secret: Copy this from the same application. Leave blank later to keep the saved secret.
data_description.username: Login of the TTLock mobile-app account that owns or administers the locks.
data_description.ttlock_password: Used once to obtain OAuth tokens and never saved. Leave blank when editing an existing connection.
data_description.ttlock_listings: Only selected listings receive TTLock codes. Locks are assigned on the next screen.

ttlock_listing.title: TTLock access for {listing}
ttlock_listing.description: Choose the doors that guests of this listing may open. The optional Guesty suffix is display-only and is never sent to TTLock.

data.guesty_code_suffix: Key shown after the Guesty code (optional)

data_description.lock_ids: Select one to six locks. Every selected lock receives the same reservation PIN and validity period.
data_description.guesty_code_suffix: For example #, * or ☑️. Up to 8 visible non-numeric characters; leave empty if the PIN alone is sufficient. This setting is shared with Loxone for the same listing; for different keypads you can enter a combined hint such as ☑️ / #.

error.invalid_branding_url: Logo and favicon must use valid direct HTTPS URLs
error.same_lock: The same lock cannot be assigned twice
error.invalid_code_suffix: The Guesty code suffix must contain at most 8 visible non-numeric characters
error.invalid_code_prefix: The PIN prefix must contain 1 or 2 digits
error.custom_field_not_found: Enter a Guesty reservation custom field name, variable, or ID
error.loxone_invalid_auth: Loxone rejected the account or it lacks User management rights
error.loxone_cannot_connect: Could not connect securely to this Loxone Miniserver
error.duplicate_loxone_server: This Miniserver connection is configured more than once
error.select_loxone_group: Select at least one Loxone user group
error.groups_from_one_server: All groups for one listing must belong to the same Miniserver
error.ttlock_invalid_region: Select a supported TTLock API region
error.ttlock_invalid_auth: TTLock rejected the Open Platform application or App account
error.ttlock_cannot_connect: Could not connect to the TTLock Open Platform API
error.ttlock_no_compatible_locks: No V4 passcode lock connected to a TTLock gateway was found
error.select_ttlock_lock: Select at least one TTLock lock
error.too_many_ttlock_locks: Select no more than six TTLock locks per listing

abort.no_listings: No Guesty listings are currently available
